title: When is the best time to visit Mongolia?
excerpt: From the green of early summer to the golden light of autumn and the drama of the Naadam season — a month-by-month guide to timing your journey.

body.heading: Spring · May to June
body.text: As the snow retreats the steppe turns green and newborn foals appear at every camp. Days are mild, crowds are thin, and the light is soft and long — ideal for photography and quiet, unhurried travel before the summer peak.

body.heading: Summer & Naadam · July
body.text: July brings the warmest weather and the national Naadam festival — the busiest and most vivid time to visit. Book early: our festival departures fill months ahead. This is peak season for a reason.

body.heading: Autumn · September
body.text: Arguably the finest month: golden light, crisp air, fewer travellers and the eagle-hunting season opening in the west. A quiet, cinematic close to the year.

quote: Come in early summer and you'll have the green steppe almost to yourself.

title: Inside the Naadam festival
excerpt: Wrestling, archery and the thunder of the horse race — a first-timer's guide.

body.heading: The three manly games
body.text: Naadam centres on wrestling, archery and horse racing — traditions that stretch back to the era of Chinggis Khaan.

body.heading: Getting the best seats
body.text: The National Stadium fills fast; our departures reserve seating for the opening ceremony and wrestling finals.

title: Packing for the Gobi
excerpt: What to bring for scorching days and freezing desert nights.

body.heading: Layer for a 40° swing
body.text: Gobi days can hit 35°C while nights drop below freezing in spring and autumn — layering is essential.

body.heading: Dust and sand
body.text: A buff or scarf and eye protection make the dune crossings far more comfortable.

title: A week with eagle hunters
excerpt: Living with a Kazakh family in the Altai during hunting season.

body.heading: Arriving in Bayan-Ölgii
body.text: The far west feels like a different country — Kazakh, Muslim, and mountainous, a world away from the central steppe.

body.heading: The hunt
body.text: Golden eagles are trained over years; watching one launch from a rider's arm across a snow-dusted valley is unforgettable.

title: What nomads eat
excerpt: Airag, aaruul and the art of the dairy year on the steppe.

body.heading: The dairy year
body.text: Summer is dairy season — airag (fermented mare's milk), aaruul (dried curd) and clotted cream all follow the herding calendar.

title: A first-timer's guide to horse trekking
excerpt: How to ride, what to expect, and why it's the best way to see the land.

body.heading: No experience needed
body.text: Mongolian horses are small, sturdy and calm — most riders are comfortable within a day.

title: 10 things you didn't know about the nomads
excerpt: Small customs and big traditions that shape daily life.

body.heading: Hospitality is sacred
body.text: A traveller is always welcomed into the ger with hot salted milk tea, no matter the hour.
